items.name: Draynor Manor and village approach
items.subtitle: A spooky early-game landmark with twisted trees, locked rooms, and a tight path into the village.
items.why: It is one of the first places that made the old map feel strange and bigger than the starter route.
items.what: Reference pass, palette moodboard, and first massing notes are waiting on the Lumbridge to Draynor road.

images.caption: Mood placeholder for interior lighting, party-room colour and early-game eccentricity.

items.name: Karamja docks and banana route
items.subtitle: A compact island landing with ships, crates, palms, and a readable route from Port Sarim.
items.why: The first boat trip should feel like a proper jump in the world rather than a disconnected set piece.
items.what: We are testing water scale, shore transitions, and how much island compression still feels right.

images.caption: Route planning view for the Port Sarim to Karamja crossing.

items.name: Varrock square and rooftops
items.subtitle: Banks, streets, roof language, market stalls, and the first dense city pass.
items.why: Varrock is the first true hub test: dense enough to feel busy, clean enough to navigate.
items.what: We blocked the central square, tested warm roof tones, and started a repeatable townhouse module.

images.caption: Current roof palette and street density test.

items.name: Grand Exchange market ring
items.subtitle: A hub zone with market stalls, circular paths, bank flow, and screenshot-friendly overlooks.
items.why: It is the social heartbeat of OSRS, so it needs to read instantly even when compressed.
items.what: The stall rhythm, central floor shape, and first donor board location are roughed in.

images.caption: Market stall layout and donor-board sightline test.

items.name: Lumbridge courtyard scale test
items.subtitle: A starter-area proof of scale for castle walls, chapel angle, paths, and bridge distance.
items.why: Lumbridge is the emotional spawn point. If it feels wrong, the whole project feels wrong.
items.what: We settled the first scale rules, chose a castle palette, and created path widths that work in Minecraft first-person.

images.caption: Completed castle-courtyard scale and path-width proof.
